directive.title: FLOWSEER-RESP-003-B: Audit and Replace Custom Floating Dropdown Components with Native-Compatible Implementations

auditor.summary: The build implements the core mobile compliance requirements (useMediaQuery hook, native select fallback, touch targets, z-index capping) and produces the required audit documentation. However, it has two critical issues: (1) the desktop rendering paths for both Select and Combobox are completely replaced with simplified implementations instead of preserving the existing Radix/Headless UI primitives, which breaks existing functionality and snapshots; (2) the TypeScript prop handling has potential type conflicts. Additionally, the Lighthouse audit criterion is not addressed at all. The build is recoverable with focused fixes to restore desktop behavior while keeping mobile changes.

top_issues.description: Desktop path renders a native <select> instead of the existing Radix Select primitive. The spec explicitly states 'On desktop, render existing Radix Select primitive unchanged.' This breaks existing functionality and snapshots.
Desktop path renders a custom button-based combobox instead of the existing Radix Popper/Headless UI implementation. The spec requires preserving existing behavior on desktop.

The ...props spread onto the native <select> element may cause TypeScript errors because the interface defines 'data-testid' as a destructured prop, but it's also included in the spread. This creates duplicate attributes.
